title: Interpret line plots
description: Practice interpreting line plots and the meaning of each point.
topic_path: Module 5: Fraction equivalence, ordering, and operations
Topic E: Extending fraction equivalence to fractions greater than 1

assessment_items.question_content:
Pietro is measuring the thickness of his favorite books. He  rounds his measurements to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{8}$ inch and records the information on the line plot below. Each point represents one book.

[[☃ image 1]]

**List the thickness of the $3$ thickest books, from greatest to least.**

Book | Thickness (inches)
-|-
Thickest book | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ thickest book | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$3^{\text{rd}}$ thickest book | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one book. The thickest books will have the points farthest to the right.

What are the thicknesses of the $3$ points farthest to the right?

The thickest book measured $2$ inches.

[[☃ image 1]]

The second and third thickest books measured $1\dfrac{6}{8}$ inches each.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the thicknesses of the $3$ thickest books.

Book | Thickness (inches)
-|-
Thickest book | $2$
$2^{\text{nd}}$ thickest book | $1\dfrac{6}{8}$
$3^{\text{rd}}$ thickest book | $1\dfrac{6}{8}$

options.alt: A line plot labeled Thickness, in inches, from 0 to 2  with tick marks every one-eighth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above the tick mark labeled one and one-eighth, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled one and two-eighths, 4 dots above the tick mark labeled one and five-eighths, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled one and six-eighths, and 1 dot above the tick mark labeled two.

assessment_items.question_content:
Luis recorded how much milk his family drank each week. He graphed the volumes, to the nearest $\dfrac14$ of a gallon, on the line plot below. Each point represents one week.

[[☃ image 2]]

**List the volumes of milk for the $3$ weeks the family drank the most milk.**

Milk | Volume (gallons)
-|-
Most milk drank | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ most milk drank | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$3^{\text{rd}}$ most milk drank  | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one week. The largest volume will be the points farthest to the right.

What is the volume of the $3$ points farthest to the right?

The largest volume of milk measured $3\dfrac{3}{4}$ gallons.

[[☃ image 1]]

The second largest volume of milk measured $3\dfrac{1}{4}$ gallons.

[[☃ image 2]]

The third largest volume of milk measured $3$ gallons.

[[☃ image 1]]

Here are the volumes of $3$ weeks the family drank the most milk.

Milk | Volume (gallons)
-|-
Most milk drank | $3\dfrac{3}{4}$
$2^{\text{nd}}$ most milk drank | $3\dfrac{1}{4}$
$3^{\text{rd}}$ most milk drank  |$3$

options.alt: A line plot labeled Thickness, in inches, from 0 to 2  with tick marks every one-eighth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above one and one-eighth, 1 dot above one and two-eighths, 4 dots above one and five-eighths, 2 dots above one and six-eighths, and 1 dot above the 2.

A line plot labeled 1 to 4 with tick marks at every one-fourth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above the tick mark labeled one, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled one and two-fourths, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled two and one-fourth, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled two and two-fourths, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled three, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled three and one-fourth, and 1 dot above the tick mark labeled three and three-fourths.

assessment_items.question_content:
Students in Mr.Badger’s science class planted $13$ conifers around the school yard. After one month, they measured the heights of the conifers.  They rounded each measurement to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of an inch and plotted the results on the line plot below. Each point represents one conifer.

[[☃ image 3]]

**List the heights of the $2$ tallest plants, from greatest to least.**

Conifer | Height (inches)
-|-
Tallest plant | [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ tallest plant | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Each point represents one plant. The tallest plants will have the points farthest to the right.

What are the heights of the $2$ points farthest to the right?

The *tallest* conifer measured $9\dfrac{2}{4}$ inches.

[[☃ image 2]]

The second tallest conifer measured $8\dfrac{3}{4}$ .inches.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the heights of the $2$ tallest plants.

Conifer | Height (inches)
-|-
Tallest plant | $9\dfrac{2}{4}$
$2^{\text{nd}}$ tallest plant | $8\dfrac{3}{4}$

options.alt: A line plot labeled Thickness, in inches, from 0 to 2  with tick marks every one-eighth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above one and one-eighth, 1 dot above one and two-eighths, 4 dots above one and five-eighths, 2 dots above one and six-eighths, and 1 dot above the 2.

A line plot labeled 3 to 7 with tick marks at each one-half unit. Tick marks 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7 are labeled. Above tick mark 3 there is one dot, Above tick mark three and one-half there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark four and one-half, there is a column of three dots. Above tick mark 5 there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark six and one-half there is one dot.

A line plot labeled 6 to 10 with tick marks at every one-fourth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above the tick mark at six and one-fourth, 2 dots above the tick mark at six and three-fourths, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled seven and two-fourths, 4 dots above the tick mark at eight and one-fourth, 2 dots above the tick mark at eight and three-fourths, and 1 dot above the tick mark labeled nine and two-fourths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The owner of a produce stand is selling bunches of grapes, priced by the pound.  She rounded each measurement to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{8}$ of a pound and plotted them on the line plot below. Each point represents one bunch of grapes.

[[☃ image 4]]

**List the weights of the $3$ lightest bunches.**

Bunch | Weight (pounds)
-|-
Lightest bunch| [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ lightest bunch | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
$3^{\text{rd}}$ lightest bunch | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
Each point represents one bunch of grapes. The lightest bunch of grapes will have the points farthest to the left.

What are the weights of the $3$ points farthest to the left?

The *lightest* bunch of grapes weighs $\dfrac58$ of a pound.

[[☃ image 3]]

The second and third lightest bunches weighed  $\dfrac68$ pounds each.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the weights of the $3$ lightest bunches of grapes.

Bunch | Weight (pounds)
-|-
Lightest bunch| $\dfrac58$
$2^{\text{nd}}$ lightest bunch | $\dfrac{6}{8}$ 
$3^{\text{rd}}$ lightest bunch | $\dfrac{6}{8}$

options.alt: A line plot labeled 0 to 2  with tick marks every one-eighth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 1 dot above the tick mark at five-eighths, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled six-eighths, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled one, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled one and four-eighths, 4 dots above the tick mark at one and five-eighths, and one dot above the tick mark at one and seven-eighths.

assessment_items.question_content:
Patrick analyzes how much sugar is in $10$ popular candy bars.  He records the data on the line plot below. Each point represents one candy bar.

[[☃ image 4]]

**List the grams of sugar of the $2$ candy bars that have the least amount of sugar.**

Candy bar | Sugar (grams)
-|-
Least amount| [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ least amount | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
Each point represents one candy bar. The least amount of sugar will be the points farthest to the left.

What is the amount of sugar of the $2$ points farthest to the left?

The *least* amount of sugar in a candy bar is $21\dfrac12$ grams of sugar.

[[☃ image 3]]

The second least amount of sugar in a candy bar is $22\dfrac{1}{2}$ grams of sugar.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the amounts of sugar in the $2$ candy bars with the least amount of sugar.

Candy bar | Sugar (grams)
-|-
Least amount| $21\dfrac12$
$2^{\text{nd}}$ least amount | $22\dfrac12$

A line plot labeled 3 to 7 with tick marks at each one-half unit. Tick marks 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7 are labeled. Above tick mark 3 there is one dot, Above tick mark three and one-half there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark four and one-half, there is a column of three dots. Above tick mark 5 there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark six and one-half there is one dot.

A line plot labeled 6 to 10 with tick marks every one-fourth unit. Above the tick mark for six and one-fourth is a column of three dots. Above the tick mark for six and three-fourths is a column of two dots. Above the tick mark for seven and two-fourths there is one dot. Above the tick mark at eight and one-fourth is a column of four dots. Above the tick mark for eight and three-fourths is a column of two dots. Above the tick mark for nine and two-fourths is one dot.

A line plot labeled 20 to 30 with tick marks every one-half unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 1 dot above the tick mark at twenty-one and one-half, 1 dot above the tick mark at twenty-two and one-half, 2 dots above the tick mark at twenty-three and one-half, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled twenty-six, 1 dot above the tick mark at twenty-eight and one-half, and 2 dots above the tick mark labeled thirty.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a science class experiment, the weights of $8$ baking soda samples were measured and recorded on the line plot below. Each point represents one sample.

[[☃ image 5]]

**List the weights of the $3$ lightest baking soda samples.**

Baking soda sample | Weight (grams)
-|-
Lightest sample | [[☃ numeric-input 5]]
$2^{\text{nd}}$ lightest sample | [[☃ numeric-input 6]]
$3^{\text{rd}}$ lightest sample | [[☃ numeric-input 7]]
hints:
Each point represents one sample. The lightest samples will be the points farthest to the left.

What is the weight of the $3$ points farthest to the left?

The two *lightest* samples of baking soda are $2\dfrac12$ grams.

[[☃ image 3]]

The third *lightest* sample of baking soda weighs $3\dfrac12$ grams.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the weights of the $3$ lightest baking soda samples.

Baking soda sample | Weight (grams)
-|-
Lightest sample | $2\dfrac12$ 
$2^{\text{nd}}$ lightest sample | $2\dfrac12$ 
$3^{\text{rd}}$ lightest sample | $3\dfrac12$

Above tick mark 3 there is one dot, Above tick mark three and one-half there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark four and one-half, there is a column of three dots. Above tick mark 5 there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark six and one-half there is one dot.

A line plot labeled 6 to 10 with tick marks every one-fourth unit. Above the tick mark for six and one-fourth is a column of three dots. Above the tick mark for six and three-fourths is a column of two dots. Above the tick mark for seven and two-fourths there is one dot. Above the tick mark at eight and one-fourth is a column of four dots. Above the tick mark for eight and three-fourths is a column of two dots. Above the tick mark for nine and two-fourths is one dot.

A line plot labeled 20 to 30 with tick marks every one-half unit. Above the tick mark for 21 and one-half, there is one dot. Above the tick mark 22 and one-half, there is one dot. Above the tick mark 23 and one-half, there is two dots. Above the tick mark 26 there is three dots. Above the tick mark 28 and one-half is one dot. Above the tick mark for thirty, there is a column of two dots.

A line plot labeled 2 to 5 with tick marks every one-half unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 2 dots above the tick mark labeled two and one-half, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled three and one-half, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled four, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled four and one-half, and 2 dots above the tick mark labeled five.

assessment_items.question_content:
The owner of a produce stand is selling baskets of peaches, priced by the kilogram.  She rounded each measurements to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{2}$ of a kilogram. Each point represents one basket.

[[☃ image 1]]

**List the masses of the heaviest and least heavy baskets.**

Basket | Mass (kilograms)
-|-
Heaviest basket | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Least heavy basket | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one basket. The heaviest basket will have the point farthest to the right. The least heavy basket will have the point farthest to the left.

What are the masses for those $2$ points?

The *heaviest* basket of peaches weighs $6\dfrac12$ kilograms.


[[☃ image 1]]

The *least heavy* basket of peaches weighs $3$ kilograms.

[[☃ image 1]]

These are the masses of the heaviest and least heavy baskets.

Basket | Mass (kilograms)
-|-
Heaviest basket | $6\dfrac{1}{2}$
Least heavy basket | $3$

options.alt: A line plot labeled mass, in kilograms, from 3 to 7 with tick marks at each one-half unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 1 dot above the tick mark labeled three, 2 dots above the tick mark at three and one-half, 3 dots above the tick mark at four and one-half, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled five, and 1 dot above the tick mark at six and one-half.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kylie surveys the students in her classroom to find out how much time they spent doing their homework last night.  The results are plotted in the line plot below.  The measurements are rounded to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of an hour. Each point represents a student.

[[☃ image 2]]

**List the shortest and longest homework times.**

Homework | Time (hours)
-|-
Shortest time| [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Longest time | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one student. The longest time spent on homework will have the point farthest to the right. The shortest time spent on homework will have the point farthest to the left.

What are the times for those $2$ points?

The *shortest* time spent doing homework was $\dfrac{2}{4}$ of an hour.


[[☃ image 2]]

The *longest* time spent doing homework was $1\dfrac34$ hours.


[[☃ image 2]]

These are the times for the least and most time spent on homework.

Homework | Time (hours)
-|-
Shortest time| $\dfrac{2}{4}$
Longest time | $1\dfrac34$

options.alt: A line plot labeled mass, in kilograms, from 3 to 7 with tick marks at each one-half unit. Tick marks 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7 are labeled. Above tick mark 3 there is one dot, Above tick mark three and one-half there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark four and one-half, there is a column of three dots. Above tick mark 5 there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark six and one-half there is one dot.

A line plot from 0 to 2 labeled from one-fourth to two with tick marks every one-fourth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 1 dot above the tick mark labeled two-fourths, 4 dots above the tick mark labeled three-fourths, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled one, 5 dots above the tick mark labeled one and one-fourth, 4 dots above the tick mark labeled one and two-fourths, and 1 dot above the tick mark labeled one and three-fourths.

assessment_items.question_content:
During science class, groups of students measure out different volumes of kerosene.  The line plot below shows the amount of kerosene used by $12$ groups, rounded to the nearest $\dfrac12\text{ mL}$. Each point represents one group.

[[☃ image 3]]

**List the volumes of the largest and smallest amount of kerosene used.**

Kerosene | Volume (milliliters)
-|-
Largest | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Smallest | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one group. The largest volume of kerosene used will have the point farthest to the right. The smallest volume of kerosene will have the point farthest to the left.

What are the times for those $2$ points?

The *largest* amount of kerosene used was $7\dfrac12 \text{ mL}$.

[[☃ image 4]]

The *smallest* amount of kerosene used from $4\dfrac{1}{2}\text{ mL}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

These are the volumes for the largest and smallest amount of kerosene used.

Kerosene | Volume (milliliters)
-|-
Largest | $7\dfrac12$.
Smallest | $4\dfrac{1}{2}$

Above tick mark 3 there is one dot, Above tick mark three and one-half there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark four and one-half, there is a column of three dots. Above tick mark 5 there is a column of two dots. Above tick mark six and one-half there is one dot.

A line plot labeled 0 to 2 with tick marks every one-fourth unit. Above the tick mark at two-fourths is one dot. Above the tick mark at three-fourths is a column of four dots. Above the tick mark at one is a column of two dots. Above the tick mark at one and one-fourth is a column of five dots. Above the tick mark at one and two-fourths is a column of four dots. Above the tick mark at one and three-fourths is one dot.

A line plot labeled 4 to 8 with tick marks every one-half unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 2 dots above the tick mark labeled four and one-half, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled five, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled six and one-half, 2 dots above the tick mark labeled seven, and 3 dots above the tick mark labeled seven and one-half.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scientists in Florida are measuring the masses of sea turtles as they hatch.  They create a line graph and plot the masses of $18$ turtles, rounded to the nearest $\dfrac14$ of an ounce. Each point represents a turtle.

[[☃ image 5]]

**List the weights of the sea turtles with the lightest and heaviest mass.**

Sea Turtles | Weight (ounces)
-|-
Lightest | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Heaviest | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one turtle. The heaviest turtles will have the point farthest to the right. The lightest turtles will have the point farthest to the left.

What are the times for those $2$ points?

The *lightest* turtle weighed $12\dfrac{1}{4}\text{ oz}$.

[[☃ image 3]]

The *heaviest* turtle weighed $13\dfrac{3}{4}\text{ oz}$.

[[☃ image 5]]

These are the weights for the lightest and heaviest turtles.

Sea Turtles | Weight (ounces)
-|-
Lightest | $12\dfrac{1}{4}$
Heaviest | $13\dfrac{3}{4}$

Above tick mark seven and one-half, there is a column of three dots.

A line plot labeled from 12 to 14 with tick marks every one-fourth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 3 dots above the tick mark labeled twelve and one-fourth, 4 dots above the tick mark labeled twelve and two-fourths, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled thirteen, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled thirteen and one-fourth, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled thirteen and two-fourths, and 2 dots above the tick mark labeled thirteen dn three-fourths.

assessment_items.question_content:
The weights of $11$ different babies are recorded on the line plot below.  Each weight was rounded to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{8}$ pound. Each point represents a baby.


[[☃ image 6]]

**List the weights of the lightest and heaviest baby.**

Baby | Weight (pounds)
-|-
Lightest | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Heaviest | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one baby. The lightest baby will have the point farthest to the left. The heaviest baby will have the point farthest to the right. 

What are the times for those $2$ points?

The *lightest* baby weighs $6\dfrac{6}{8}$ pounds.

[[☃ image 4]]

The *heaviest* baby weighs $9\dfrac{2}{8}$ pounds.

[[☃ image 6]]

These are the weights for the lightest and heaviest babies.

Baby | Weight (pounds)
-|-
Lightest | $6\dfrac{6}{8}$
Heaviest | $9\dfrac{2}{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
For $7$ days, Jiang recorded the number of hours he slept each night.  He rounded each time to the nearest $\dfrac{1}{4}$ of an hour and plotted it on the line plot below. Each point represents one night.

[[☃ image 7]]

**List the hours of sleep for the nights Jiang slept the least and most.**

Sleep | Time (hours)
-|-
Least sleep | [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
Most sleep | [[☃ numeric-input 3]]
hints:
Each point represents one nights sleep. The most sleep will have the point farthest to the right. The least amount of sleep will have the point farthest to the left.

What are the times for those $2$ points?

Jiang slept the *least* on one of the nights he slept $7\dfrac{1}{4}$ hours.

[[☃ image 5]]

Jiang slept the *most* on the night he slept $8\dfrac{2}{4}$ hours.

[[☃ image 7]]

These are the times for the least amount of sleep and the most amount of sleep.

Sleep | Time (hours)
-|-
Least sleep | $7\dfrac{1}{4}$
Most sleep| $8\dfrac{2}{4}$

options.alt: A line plot labeled Sleep, in hours, from 7 to 9 with tick marks at every one-fourth unit. Dots are plotted as follows: 2 dots above the tick mark labeled seven and one-fourth, 3 dots above the tick mark labeled seven and three-fourths, 1 dot above the tick mark labeled eight and one-fourth, and 1 dot above the tick mark labeled eight and two-fourths.
